title: teucrium buxifolium var. rigualii
intro: Teucrium buxifolium var. rigualii, also known as a variety of the shrubby germander plant, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to certain regions. The medicinal properties of Teucrium buxifolium var. rigualii include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, as well as its ability to reduce fever and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Teucrium buxifolium var. rigualii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for use in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
